name: Rabbit R1
short_description: A small handheld AI device with a screen, camera, scroll wheel, and push-to-talk assistant.
long_description: A pocket gadget that packages a voice-first AI assistant into a dedicated handheld object rather than an app. Its standalone-device form is a bet that a purpose-built carrier can feel distinct from a phone, even while overlapping heavily with one.

images.caption: Rabbit R1 handheld AI device.
images.alt_text: A person holds up a small bright-orange Rabbit R1 handheld AI device toward the camera, its square color screen displaying the number 640.

interactions.title: Turn a boring snapshot into retro AI art
interactions.description: Point the R1's rotating camera at whatever's in front of you, double-press the side button, and watch the photo come back as a pixelated, retro-futuristic image saved to the device's Magic Gallery.

interactions.title: Hold up a meeting and walk away with a summary
interactions.description: Use it as a dedicated recorder: capture long-form dictation or a conversation and get back a clean transcript with filler words stripped and an AI-generated summary.

interactions.title: Teach it a chore once, let it repeat it
interactions.description: In teach mode, you demonstrate a web task — log in, click through, fill a form — once in the browser, and the R1 learns to carry out that same workflow on its own afterward.

interactions.title: Talk a little game into existence
interactions.description: With rabbitOS 2's 'creations,' you describe a tool or game to the Rabbit intern out loud and it builds a small working app right on the device within minutes.

interactions.title: Cracked open and turned into a research target
interactions.description: A reverse-engineering community calling itself Rabbitude pried into the codebase and surfaced hardcoded API keys for services like ElevenLabs, Azure, and Google Maps — keys that, they showed, could read past responses or brick devices.

interactions.title: Running on a phone it was meant to replace
interactions.description: A tipster shared the launcher APK and a reviewer sideloaded it onto a Pixel 6a, mapping the volume key to the R1's push-to-talk button and stepping through setup — making the case that the dedicated gadget was really an Android app in a plastic shell.

interactions.title: Identify a plant on the trail, then log it
interactions.description: Point the camera at a flower mid-walk to identify it by voice, then chain a taught web routine to drop the find into a personal spreadsheet — combining its vision input with a teach-mode automation it already supports.
